# AI FAQ Receptionist Upsell Playbook **Target Audience:** Existing AI FAQ Receptionist customers **Goal:** Convert current users to premium add‑ons (Multilingual Support, Analytics Dashboard, Custom Integrations) while reinforcing value and minimizing churn. --- ## 1. Positioning Framework | Premium Add‑On | Core Value Proposition | Business Impact | Ideal Customer Segment | |----------------|-----------------------|----------------|------------------------| | **Multilingual Support** | Extend the AI FAQ Receptionist to serve customers in **≥ 5 additional languages** with native‑level accuracy. | • Increase global reach → +10 % average ticket volume.
• Reduce reliance on human translators → ‑15 % support cost. | Companies with international user bases or plans to enter new markets. | | **Analytics Dashboard** | Real‑time insights on FAQ usage, intent trends, and resolution rates. | • Identify knowledge gaps → +20 % FAQ coverage.
• Optimize bot scripts → ‑12 % average handling time. | Product‑focused teams that need data‑driven decisions. | | **Custom Integrations** | Seamless connectivity to CRM, ticketing, and ERP systems via API or pre‑built connectors. | • Automate ticket creation → ‑30 % manual effort.
• Enrich bot context → +18 % first‑contact resolution. | Enterprises with mature tech stacks seeking workflow automation. | **Key Message:** “Your AI FAQ Receptionist already answers questions—let’s make it *speak* your customers’ language, *show* you what they need, and *talk* to the tools you already use.” --- ## 2. Pricing Suggestions Pricing is tiered by **annual contract** to encourage longer commitments. All prices are **USD** and **ex‑VAT**. | Add‑On | Base Price (per month) | Discount for 12‑mo term | Discount for 24‑mo term | Total Annual Revenue (ARR) | |--------|-----------------------|--------------------------|--------------------------|----------------------------| | Multilingual Support (up to 5 languages) | $500 | 10 % → $5400 | 20 % → $4800 | $5,400 (12 mo) / $4,800 (24 mo) | | Analytics Dashboard | $350 | 10 % → $3780 | 20 % → $3360 | $3,780 / $3,360 | | Custom Integrations (up to 3 connectors) | $650 | 10 % → $7020 | 20 % → $6240 | $7,020 / $6,240 | **Bundled Offer (All 3 Add‑Ons):** - **Base bundle price:** $1,400 / month - **12‑mo discount:** 15 % → $14,280 ARR - **24‑mo discount:** 25 % → $12,600 ARR *Assumption:* 70 % of existing customers have a baseline spend of $1,000/mo on the core AI FAQ Receptionist. Upsell conversion rates are projected at 12 % for single add‑ons and 5 % for the full bundle (see Section 4). --- ## 3. Upsell Process Flow 1. **Data Review (Quarterly)** – Pull usage metrics from the existing dashboard. Identify: - % of sessions from non‑English locales. - FAQ topics with > 15 % “I don’t know” fallback. - Integration gaps (e.g., tickets not auto‑created). 2. **Opportunity Scoring** – Assign a **Score = (Locale % × 0.4) + (Fallback % × 0.3) + (Integration Gap % × 0.3)**. - **Score ≥ 0.6** → Prioritize for premium pitch. 3. **Personalized Outreach** – Account‑based email + calendar invite for a 30‑min “Value Review”. Include a one‑pager with the relevant add‑on’s ROI estimate (see Section 4). 4. **Demo & ROI Calculator** – Walk through a live demo of the add‑on, then run the calculator: \[ \text{Projected Net Benefit} = (\text{Revenue Increase} - \text{Cost of Add‑On}) \times \text{Adoption Rate} \] Example for Multilingual Support: - Current monthly ticket volume: 2,000 - Expected international ticket lift: **10 %** → 200 extra tickets - Avg. ticket value: $15 → $3,000 additional revenue/mo - Add‑On cost (annualized): $5400 / 12 = $450/mo - **Net Benefit** = $3,000 – $450 = **$2,550/mo** 5. **Close** – Offer the appropriate term discount and bundle incentive. --- ## 4. ROI Calculations (Sample Scenarios) | Scenario | Baseline (Core) Rev. | Add‑On Cost (annual) | Expected Revenue Lift | Net Annual Gain | |----------|----------------------|----------------------|-----------------------|-----------------| | **Multilingual – Mid‑Size SaaS** | $120,000 | $5,400 | +$36,000 (10 % lift) | **+$30,600** | | **Analytics – E‑commerce** | $120,000 | $3,780 | +$24,000 (20 % lift from better FAQ coverage) | **+$20,220** | | **Custom Integration – FinTech** | $120,000 | $7,020 | +$45,600 (30 % lift from automated ticketing) | **+$38,580** | | **Full Bundle – Global Enterprise** | $120,000 | $14,280 | +$84,000 (combined lifts) | **+$69,720** | *Assumptions:* - Adoption Rate = 80 % (customers use the add‑on for ≥ 80 % of eligible interactions). - Revenue Lift percentages derived from industry benchmarks (Gartner 2024 “AI Support ROI”). --- ## 5. Objection Handling Matrix | Objection | Underlying Concern | Response Strategy | Sample Reply | |-----------|--------------------|-------------------|--------------| | **“It’s too expensive.”** | Cost vs. budget constraints. | Emphasize **net gain** and **discounted term**. Show ROI calculator. | “With the 12‑month discount, the add‑on costs $450/mo but unlocks an estimated $2,550/mo net benefit—essentially a **5× return**.” | | **“We don’t need more languages now.”** | Perceived lack of demand. | Highlight **latent demand** from usage data (non‑English sessions). | “Your logs show 12 % of sessions originate from Spanish‑speaking users. Adding multilingual support could capture that revenue now rather than later.” | | **“Our team can’t manage another integration.”** | Resource constraints. | Offer **managed‑integration service** (included in first‑year). | “Our integration team will handle the setup at no extra charge for the first year, so you won’t need internal resources.” | | **“We already have analytics tools.”** | Redundancy concern. | Position the dashboard as **AI‑specific** and **real‑time**. | “Unlike generic analytics, our dashboard correlates FAQ intent with bot performance, giving you actionable insights you can’t get elsewhere.” | | **“We’re not ready to commit to a longer term.”** | Commitment anxiety. | Provide a **pilot** (30‑day trial) with a **monthly‑pay** option. | “You can start with a month‑to‑month plan; if the ROI meets expectations, we’ll lock in the annual discount.” | --- ## 6. Sample Conversation Scripts ### 6.1. Multilingual Support | Stage | Script | |-------|--------| | **Opening** | “Hi [Customer Name], I noticed 12 % of your FAQ traffic comes from Spanish‑speaking users. Have you considered expanding support to those visitors?” | | **Value Pitch** | “Our Multilingual add‑on lets the AI respond in up to five languages with native accuracy. For a client similar to you, it generated an extra $3,000 in monthly revenue.” | | **Objection** | *Customer:* “We don’t have the budget.” | | **Response** | “The add‑on costs $450 per month after the 12‑month discount, but the projected net benefit is $2,550 per month—essentially a **5× ROI**. Plus, we can start with a 30‑day trial at the monthly rate, no long‑term commitment.” | | **Close** | “Shall we schedule a quick demo next Tuesday to see the language switch in action?” | ### 6.2. deliverable.content:
# Healthcare Cold‑Outreach Email Sequence  
**Product:** AI FAQ Receptionist (AI‑Powered, HIPAA‑Compliant, 24/7 Patient Support)  
**Target:** Decision‑makers at outpatient clinics, hospitals, and specialty practices (e.g., Practice Managers, CEOs, Directors of Patient Experience)  

---  

## 1. Overview  

| Step | Timing (relative to first send) | Goal | Core Benefit Highlight |
|------|--------------------------------|------|------------------------|
| 1️⃣ | Day 0 (initial outreach) | Introduce the AI FAQ Receptionist and spark curiosity | Immediate reduction in call‑center volume + HIPAA compliance |
| 2️⃣ | Day 3 (first follow‑up) | Reinforce ROI with a quick case study | 30 % drop in call‑wait times within 30 days |
| 3️⃣ | Day 7 (second follow‑up) | Address common objections (security, integration) | Seamless EMR integration, no PHI exposure |
| 4️⃣ | Day 14 (third follow‑up) | Offer a live demo / pilot | Zero‑cost 14‑day pilot, measurable KPIs |
| 5️⃣ | Day 21 (final follow‑up) | Create urgency & close the loop | Limited‑time “early‑adopter” discount |

All emails are **personalized** with dynamic tokens (see Section 2) and **concise** (150‑200 words) to respect busy clinicians’ time.  

---  

## 2. Personalization Tokens  

| Token | Example Value | Placement |
|-------|---------------|-----------|
| `{{FirstName}}` | Dr. Emily | Subject line & greeting |
| `{{LastName}}` | Chen | Greeting (optional) |
| `{{Title}}` | Practice Manager | Greeting |
| `{{PracticeName}}` | Sunrise Family Medicine | Opening sentence |
| `{{Specialty}}` | Cardiology | Body copy where relevant |
| `{{Location}}` | Austin, TX | Footer or contextual reference |
| `{{RecentAward}}` | “Best Patient Experience 2023” | Optional – pulled from public data |
| `{{ContactMethod}}` | phone | CTA “reply to this email or call me at …” |

*All tokens are resolved by the CRM at send time.*  

---  

## 3. Email Templates  

### **Email 1 – Introduction**  
**Subject:** {{FirstName}}, a HIPAA‑safe way to answer patient FAQs 24/7  

**Body:**  

```
Hi {{FirstName}},

I’m Maya Patel, Head of Partnerships at MedAssist AI. I noticed {{PracticeName}} recently earned {{RecentAward}} – congratulations on delivering such a high‑quality patient experience!

We’ve built an AI FAQ Receptionist that sits on your website and phone system, instantly answering common questions (e.g., “What are your office hours?” or “How do I prepare for my colonoscopy?”). Because it never stores PHI, it’s fully HIPAA‑compliant and reduces the volume of routine calls by up to 35 % within the first month.

Would you be open to a 5‑minute chat this week to see how it could free up your front‑desk staff for more complex tasks?

Best,  
Maya Patel  
Senior Partnerships Manager, MedAssist AI  
📞 555‑123‑4567 | ✉️ maya.patel@medassist.ai
```

**Timing Recommendation:** Send at 10 am local time on a Tuesday or Wednesday (optimal open rates for healthcare execs).  

---  

### **Email 2 – Social Proof & Quick ROI**  
**Subject:** {{FirstName}}, see how {{PracticeName}} could cut call‑wait time by 30 %  

**Body:**  

```
Hi {{FirstName}},

I wanted to share a brief story from a clinic similar to {{PracticeName}} (a 12‑physician family practice in {{Location}}). After deploying our AI FAQ Receptionist, they saw:

* 30 % reduction in average call‑wait time within 30 days  
* 22 % increase in patient satisfaction scores (Press Ganey)  
* No additional staffing costs – the AI runs 24/7 at a flat $199/mo

All of this was achieved while staying fully HIPAA‑compliant because the AI never stores or transmits PHI.

Could we schedule a quick call to explore whether those results are realistic for {{PracticeName}}?

Thanks,  
Maya Patel  
📞 555‑123‑4567 | ✉️ maya.patel@medassist.ai
```

**Timing Recommendation:** Send 72 hours after Email 1 (Day 3).  

---  

### **Email 3 – Objection Handling (Security & Integration)**  
**Subject:** {{FirstName}}, addressing security & EMR integration concerns  

**Body:**  

```
Hi {{FirstName}},

I understand security and workflow integration are top priorities for {{PracticeName}}. Here’s how our AI FAQ Receptionist handles both:

1. **HIPAA‑by‑design:** The AI never records or stores any PHI. All interactions are encrypted in transit and discarded after the session.  
2. **Zero‑touch EMR integration:** Our connector pulls only non‑PHI schedule data (e.g., appointment types, provider names) via your existing API, so there’s no custom coding required.  
3. **Compliance audit ready:** We provide a quarterly compliance report you can share with your legal team.

If you’d like a deeper dive into the technical details, I’m happy to set up a 15‑minute call with our CTO, Alex Rivera.

Looking forward to your thoughts,  
Maya Patel  
📞 555‑123‑4567 | ✉️ maya.patel@medassist.ai
```

**Timing Recommendation:** Send 4 days after Email 2 (Day 7).  

---  

### **Email 4 – Invitation to Live Demo / Pilot**  
**Subject:** {{FirstName}}, try the AI FAQ Receptionist risk‑free for 14 days  

**Body:**  

```
Hi {{FirstName}},

Would you like to see the AI FAQ Receptionist in action on your own site? We’re offering a **no‑cost, 14‑day pilot** for {{PracticeName}} that includes:

* Full deployment on your website and phone line  
* Real‑time analytics dashboard (call deflection, patient satisfaction)  
* Dedicated onboarding specialist  

After the pilot, you’ll have concrete data to decide whether to roll it out practice‑wide. I can set up a live demo at a time that works for you – how does {{ProposedTime}} sound?

Best regards,  
Maya Patel  
📞 555‑123‑4567 | ✉️ maya.patel@medassist.ai
```

*Replace `{{ProposedTime}}` with two optional slots (e.g., “Tuesday 10 am EST or Thursday 2 pm EST”).*  

**Timing Recommendation:** Send 7 days after Email 3 (Day 14).  

---  

### **Email 5 – Final Call & Limited‑Time Offer**  
**Subject:** {{FirstName}}, last chance: early‑adopter discount expires soon  

**Body:**  

```
Hi {{FirstName}},

I wanted to give you one final heads‑up: we’re closing our early‑adopter program next Friday. If {{PracticeName}} signs up before then, you’ll lock in a **20 % discount** on the first year’s subscription and receive priority support.

Given the compliance, efficiency, and patient‑experience gains we’ve discussed, I think this could be a strategic win for {{PracticeName}}.

If you’re interested, just reply “Yes” and I’ll send the agreement and onboarding steps right away.

Thank you for considering MedAssist AI – I hope we can partner to make patient communication smoother for your team.

Warmly,  
Maya Patel  
📞 555‑123‑4567 | ✉️ maya.patel@medassist.ai
```

**Timing Recommendation:** Send 7 days after Email 4 (Day 21).  

---  

## 4. Follow‑Up Timing Summary  

| Day | Action | Reason |
|-----|--------|--------|
| 0   | Email 1 (Intro) | Capture attention when inboxes are fresh. |
| 3   | Email 2 (Social proof) | Reinforce value after initial curiosity. |
| 7   | Email 3 (Objection handling) | Address security & integration concerns before they become blockers. |
| 14  | Email 4 (Demo / Pilot) | Give decision‑makers time to consider and schedule a demo. |
| 21  | Email 5 (Final offer) | Create urgency with a limited‑time discount. |

*All sends should be timed to the recipient’s local business hours (10 am–12 pm) and tracked for opens/clicks. If an email is opened but not replied, consider a brief “quick‑question” SMS (if consented) on the same day.*  

---  

## 5. Measurement & Success Criteria  

| Metric | Target | Measurement Tool |
|--------|--------|-------------------|
| Open Rate (Email 1) | ≥ 35 % | CRM email analytics |
| Reply Rate (any step) | ≥ 12 % | CRM inbound tracking |
| Demo / Pilot Sign‑ups | ≥ 4 per 100 contacts | CRM pipeline stage |
| Conversion (Pilot → Paid) | ≥ 50 % | Subscription data |
| Average Time to Close | ≤ 45 days from Email 1 | CRM reporting |

If any metric falls below target after two weeks, adjust timing (e.g., test Thursday sends) or tweak subject lines using A/B testing.  

---  

## 6. Optional Add‑Ons  

| Add‑On | Description | When to Deploy |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| **One‑pager PDF** | One‑page visual of compliance workflow & ROI chart | Attach to Email 2 or Email 4 |
| **Video Snippet** | 30‑second walkthrough of AI answering a patient question | Embed in Email 4 (demo invite) |
| **Referral Incentive** | $100 credit for each practice that refers another | Mention in Email 5 for added urgency |

---  

## 7. Full Sequence at a Glance  

```
Day 0   → Email 1 – Intro (subject: “{{FirstName}}, a HIPAA‑safe way to answer patient FAQs 24/7”)
Day 3   → Email 2 – Social proof (subject: “{{FirstName}}, see how {{PracticeName}} could cut call‑wait time by 30 %”)
Day 7   → Email 3 – Objection handling (subject: “{{FirstName}}, addressing security & EMR integration concerns”)
Day 14  → Email 4 – Demo / Pilot (subject: “{{FirstName}}, try the AI FAQ Receptionist risk‑free for 14 days”)
Day 21  → Ema

deliverable.content:
# Monthly FAQ Knowledge‑Base Refresh SOP  
**Document ID:** AUTO‑027  
**Effective Date:** 2026‑07‑01  
**Version:** 1.0  

---

## 1. Purpose  

To ensure that the organization’s public and internal FAQ knowledge‑base remains accurate, relevant, and aligned with current product, policy, and support realities. This SOP defines the end‑to‑end monthly process for:

1. **Collecting** new or revised FAQ items from stakeholders.  
2. **Updating** the knowledge‑base content in the centralized repository.  
3. **Publishing** the refreshed FAQ to all consumer‑facing channels.  

The procedure also includes a rollback plan to restore the previous version should any issue arise after publishing.

---

## 2. Scope  

- **Applies to:** All teams that contribute to the FAQ (Product, Support, Legal, Marketing, and Operations).  
- **Systems Covered:**  
  - Central FAQ repository: `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/faq/` (source: SOUL.md)  
  - Content Management System (CMS): **Confluence** (space `FAQ`)  
  - Public portal: **Help Center** (hosted on Zendesk)  
- **Frequency:** First business day of each month (e.g., 2026‑07‑01, 2026‑08‑01, …).  

---

## 3. Roles & Responsibilities  

| Role | Primary Owner | Key Responsibilities |
|------|----------------|----------------------|
| **FAQ Owner** | Metis (Org Excellence Lead) 🧠 | Approves final content, triggers publishing, oversees rollback. |
| **FAQ Coordinator** | Operations Analyst (e.g., Alex Rivera) | Collects submissions, runs content validation, maintains version control. |
| **Subject‑Matter Experts (SMEs)** | Product Manager, Support Lead, Legal Counsel, Marketing Lead | Provide new/updated FAQ items, review wording for accuracy and compliance. |
| **CMS Administrator** | IT Services – Platform Engineer (e.g., Priya Patel) | Performs CMS import, verifies formatting, and publishes to Confluence. |
| **Portal Publisher** | Customer Experience Specialist (e.g., Maya Liu) | Mirrors Confluence updates to Zendesk Help Center, runs post‑publish QA. |
| **Rollback Lead** | Operations Analyst (same as Coordinator) | Executes rollback steps if publishing defects are detected. |

All participants must sign off on the **Monthly FAQ Sign‑off Sheet** (Google Sheet: `FAQ‑Monthly‑Signoff-2026`) before the publishing step.

---

## 4. Tools & Artefacts  

| Tool | Location / Access | Use |
|------|-------------------|-----|
| **FAQ Repository** | `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/faq/` | Source of truth for raw markdown files. |
| **Confluence Space** | `https://confluence.company.com/display/FAQ` | Internal knowledge‑base for staff. |
| **Zendesk Help Center** | `https://support.company.com/hc/en-us/categories/FAQ` | Public‑facing FAQ portal. |
| **Google Form – FAQ Submission** | `https://forms.gle/faq-submission-2026` | Collects new/updated items from SMEs. |
| **Google Sheet – FAQ Sign‑off** | `https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/faq-signoff-2026` | Tracks approvals. |
| **Git (internal)** | `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/.git` | Version control for markdown files. |
| **Rollback Script** | `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/scripts/faq_rollback.sh` | Automated revert to previous tag. |
| **Email Distribution List** | `faq-updates@company.com` | Notifies stakeholders of publish & rollback events. |

---

## 5. Definitions  

- **FAQ Item:** A single question‑answer pair, stored as a markdown file (`*.md`).  
- **Release Tag:** Git tag in the format `faq-YYYYMM` (e.g., `faq-202607`).  
- **Rollback Point:** The most recent successful release tag prior to the current publish.  

---

## 6. Process Overview  

```
+-------------------+      +-------------------+      +-------------------+
| 1. Collect Items  | ---> | 2. Validate &     | ---> | 3. Version &      |
|    (Form)         |      |    Consolidate    |      |    Commit         |
+-------------------+      +-------------------+      +-------------------+
        |                         |                         |
        v                         v                         v
+-------------------+      +-------------------+      +-------------------+
| 4. Review & Sign‑| ---> | 5. Publish to     | ---> | 6. Post‑Publish QA|
|    off (Sheet)    |      |    Confluence &   |      |    (Portal)       |
+-------------------+      |    Zendesk        |      +-------------------+
                           +-------------------+
                                   |
                                   v
                           +-------------------+
                           | 7. Monitor &      |
                           |    Rollback (if)  |
                           +-------------------+
```

---

## 7. Detailed Step‑by‑Step Procedure  

### 7.1. Collect New FAQ Items (Day 1 – 09:00 – 12:00)

1. **Open the Google Form** (`FAQ Submission`) and share the link with all SMEs.  
2. **SMEs submit** up to 10 new or revised items, providing:  
   - Question (max 150 chars)  
   - Answer (markdown‑compatible)  
   - Source reference (e.g., ticket ID, policy doc)  
   - Effective date (must be ≤ publish date)  
3. **Coordinator** exports the form responses to CSV (`/tmp/faq_submissions_YYYYMMDD.csv`).  

### 7.2. Validate & Consolidate (Day 1 – 13:00 – 16:00)

1. **Run validation script**:  
   ```bash
   /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/scripts/validate_faq.sh \
       /tmp/faq_submissions_$(date +%Y%m%d).csv
   ```  
   - Checks for duplicate questions, markdown syntax errors, and missing source refs.  
2. **Resolve any validation failures** within the same day by contacting the originating SME.  
3. **Merge approved items** into the repository:  
   - Create a new markdown file per item (`question_slug.md`).  
   - Place files under `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/faq/` following the folder hierarchy `category/`.  
4. **Commit changes**:  
   ```bash
   cd /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/
   git add faq/
   git commit -m "faq-$(date +%Y%m) – add 7 new items, update 3"
   ```

### 7.3. Version & Tag (Day 2 – 09:00 – 10:00)

1. **Create a release tag**:  
   ```bash
   git tag -a faq-$(date +%Y%m) -m "Monthly FAQ refresh – $(date +%Y-%m)"
   git push origin faq-$(date +%Y%m)
   ```  
2. **Record tag** in the **FAQ Release Log** (`/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/faq_release_log.xlsx`).  

### 7.4. Review & Sign‑off (Day 2 – 10:30 – 12:00)

1. **Coordinator** posts a summary of changes (link to GitHub diff) in the **FAQ Sign‑off Sheet**.  
2. **SMEs** review their items; **Legal** reviews compliance statements.  
3. **All approvers** tick the “Approved” column.  
4. **If any item is rejected**, the Coordinator returns to step 7.2 for revision.  

### 7.5. Publish to Confluence (Day 2 – 13:00 – 14:30)

1. **CMS Administrator** pulls the tagged release:  
   ```bash
   git checkout faq-$(date +%Y%m)
   ```  
2. **Export markdown to HTML** using the internal converter (`md2html.py`).  
3. **Import HTML** into the Confluence FAQ space via the Confluence REST API (script `publish_to_confluence.sh`).  
4. **Set page permissions** to “View – All Employees”.  

### 7.6. Mirror to Zendesk Help Center (Day 2 – 15:00 – 16:30)

1. **Portal Publisher** runs the Zendesk sync script:  
   ```bash
   /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/scripts/faq_zendesk_sync.sh \
       faq-$(date +%Y%m)
   ```  
2. **Verify** that each question appears correctly on the public portal (preview mode).  

### 7.7. Post‑Publish QA (Day 3 – 09:00 – 11:00)

1. **Operations Lead** conducts a spot‑check of 10 random items:  
   - Confirm question wording matches source.  
   - Verify hyperlinks open correctly.  
   - Ensure no HTML rendering errors.  
2. **Document findings** in the **Post‑Publish QA Log** (`/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/org-excellence/qa_log_YYYYMM.md`).  

### 7.8. Monitoring & Issue Handling (Day 3 – 12:00 – End of Day)

- **Monitor** the Help Center analytics for 24 hours for spikes in “No results found” queries.  
- **If critical errors** (broken links, incorrect answers) are detected, initiate the rollback plan (Section 8).  

### 7.9. Communication (Day 3 – 17:00)

- **Send email** to `faq-updates@company.com` summarizing:  
  - Release tag (`faq-YYYYMM`)  
  - Number of new/updated items  
  - Link to Confluence FAQ page  
  - Link to public Help Center page  
- **Post announcement** on the internal Slack channel `#knowledge-base`.  

---

## 8. Rollback Plan  

**Trigger:** Any of the following occurs within 48 hours of publishing:  
- Broken page rendering in Confluence or Zendesk.  
- Legal/compliance issue identified.  
- Customer‑reported misinformation.  

### 8.1. Immediate Actions  

1. **Notify** the FAQ Owner and Rollback Lead via email and Slack.  
2. **Freeze** further publishing attempts for the current cycle.  

### 8.2. Revert to Previous T

deliverable.content:
# Data Retention & Deletion Policy for FAQ Receptionist  
**Version:** 1.0  
**Effective Date:** 2026‑07‑01  
**Review Date:** 2027‑07‑01  
**Prepared by:** Nemesis – Auditor/Sentinel (Security & Compliance)  
**Document ID:** POL‑FAQ‑001  

---  

## 1. Purpose  

This policy defines how the **FAQ Receptionist** system (the “System”) retains, archives, and securely deletes client‑submitted Frequently‑Asked‑Question (FAQ) data. The policy ensures compliance with the **General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)** (EU) and the **Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)** (US) where protected health information (PHI) is involved.  

## 2. Scope  

- **Applicable Data:** All user‑generated content captured by the FAQ Receptionist, including:  
  - Question text, answer drafts, and metadata (timestamps, user identifiers).  
  - Attachments (images, PDFs) uploaded with questions.  
  - Any personal data (e.g., name, email) or PHI contained in the content.  
- **Systems Covered:** Production, staging, and backup environments of the FAQ Receptionist hosted on **AWS RDS (PostgreSQL)** and **S3**.  
- **Exclusions:** System logs, analytics data, and configuration files that do not contain user‑generated content are governed by separate retention policies.  

## 3. Legal & Regulatory Requirements  

| Requirement | Source | Application to FAQ Receptionist |
|-------------|--------|---------------------------------|
| Right to be Forgotten – deletion upon request | GDPR Art. 17 | Must delete all personal data on user request unless a lawful retention exception applies. |
| Minimum retention for medical records | HIPAA 45 C.F.R. § 164.530(b) | PHI must be retained ≥ 6 years from the date of creation. |
| Data minimisation & purpose limitation | GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) & (b) | Retain only data necessary for the purpose of providing FAQ services. |
| Secure disposal of electronic media | HIPAA 45 C.F.R. § 164.310(d)(2)(i) | Use cryptographic erasure for all storage media. |
| Documentation of retention & deletion | GDPR Art. 5(2) & HIPAA 45 C.F.R. § 164.316(b) | Maintain a retention schedule and audit logs. |

## 4. Retention Schedule  

| Data Type | Retention Period | Rationale | Deletion Trigger |
|-----------|------------------|-----------|------------------|
| **FAQ content without PHI** (question, answer, attachments) | **24 months** after last user interaction (view or edit) | Aligns with business need for historical analytics while limiting exposure. | Inactivity > 24 months → automated purge. |
| **FAQ content containing PHI** | **6 years** from the date the PHI was first captured | Minimum HIPAA retention; satisfies GDPR “storage limitation” when combined with purpose. | End of 6‑year period → secure deletion. |
| **User identifiers (email, name) linked to FAQ** | **24 months** (or until user requests deletion) | GDPR requires removal when no longer needed for the purpose. | Inactivity > 24 months **or** user‑initiated deletion request. |
| **Audit logs for deletion actions** | **7 years** | Supports forensic investigations and complies with HIPAA audit record retention. | Retained for 7 years regardless of data deletion. |

*All retention periods are calculated from the **last modification timestamp** stored in the `faq_questions.modified_at` column (see schema file `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/nemesis/db/schema.sql`).*  

## 5. Technical Controls  

### 5.1 Data Classification  

| Classification | Criteria | Storage Controls |
|----------------|----------|------------------|
| **Public** | No personal data or PHI. | Stored in S3 bucket `faq-public` with default encryption (AES‑256). |
| **Sensitive** | Contains personal data (e.g., name, email) but no PHI. | Stored in S3 bucket `faq-sensitive` with **KMS‑managed keys** (CMK `faq-sensitive-key`). |
| **PHI** | Any health‑related information as defined by HIPAA. | Stored in S3 bucket `faq‑phi` with **KMS‑managed keys** (CMK `faq-phi-key`) and **S3 Object Lock** (compliance mode) for the 6‑year retention period. |

### 5.2 Encryption  

- **At rest:** All buckets enforce AES‑256 server‑side encryption. PHI buckets use a dedicated CMK with rotation every 90 days.  
- **In transit:** TLS 1.3 enforced for all API endpoints (`https://api.example.com/faq`).  

### 5.3 Access Controls  

- **IAM Role `FAQAppReadWrite`** – granted `s3:GetObject`, `s3:PutObject`, `s3:DeleteObject` on `faq-sensitive` and `faq-public`.  
- **IAM Role `FAQAppPHI`** – granted same actions on `faq-phi` **only** to services that have completed a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA).  
- **Least‑privilege:** No human operator has direct S3 write/delete access; all actions are mediated through the application layer.  

### 5.4 Automated Retention & Deletion  

A **scheduled Lambda function** (`faq-retention-cleaner`) runs daily (cron `0 2 * * *`) and performs:  

1. Query `faq_questions` for records where `last_activity_at` < `now() - retention_period`.  
2. For each record:  
   - Delete associated objects from the appropriate S3 bucket using **S3 DeleteObject** with **`x-amz-mfa`** header (MFA‑Delete enabled).  
   - Remove the row from the database.  
   - Write an entry to the `audit_log` table (see Section 6).  

The Lambda code resides in `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/nemesis/lambda/faq_retention_cleaner.py`.  

### 5.5 Secure Deletion  

- **Cryptographic Erasure:** Deleting an S3 object with MFA‑Delete triggers immediate cryptographic erasure of the underlying data blocks.  
- **Database Records:** Rows are hard‑deleted (SQL `DELETE`) and the PostgreSQL `pg_repack` extension is run weekly to reclaim storage and prevent data remnants.  

## 6. Audit & Monitoring  

| Activity | Log Destination | Retention | Review Frequency |
|----------|----------------|-----------|------------------|
| Deletion actions (who, when, what) | `audit_log` table (`/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/workers/nemesis/db/audit_log.sql`) | 7 years | Quarterly review by Compliance Officer. |
| Access to PHI bucket | AWS CloudTrail (S3 Data Events) | 7 years | Continuous monitoring; alerts on anomalous patterns. |
| Lambda execution status | Amazon CloudWatch Logs (`/aws/lambda/faq-retention-cleaner`) | 1 year | Daily health check; failures escalated to Incident Response. |

### 6.1 Audit Procedure  

1. **Extract** all `audit_log` entries for the prior quarter.  
2. **Validate** that each deletion entry matches a corresponding `DELETE` statement in the PostgreSQL write‑ahead log (WAL).  
3. **Confirm** that the S3 object version IDs recorded in the log are no longer listed in the bucket (via `aws s3api list-object-versions`).  
4. **Report** any mismatches to the Data Protection Officer (DPO) within 5 business days.  

All audit reports are stored in the secure SharePoint folder `Compliance/FAQRetention/` (access restricted to DPO and senior management).  

## 7. Data Subject Rights & Incident Response  

- **Right to Erasure:** Upon receipt of a GDPR erasure request, the `faq‑deletion‑request` API (`POST /faq/erase`) triggers immediate deletion of the user’s data, bypassing the retention schedule. The request is logged with a `request_id` and the operation is audited as per Section 6.  
- **HIPAA Breach Notification:** If a PHI deletion fails (e.g., Lambda error), the incident is escalated to the HIPAA Incident Response Team, and a breach notification is prepared within 60 days as required by 45 C.F.R. § 164.404.  

## 8. Roles & Responsibilities  

| Role | Responsibility |
|------|----------------|
| **Data Protection Officer (DPO)** | Approve retention periods; review quarterly audit reports; handle GDPR subject‑access requests. |
| **HIPAA Compliance Officer** | Ensure PHI handling meets 45 C.F.R. § 164.530‑§ 164.540; sign off on BAA for any third‑party service. |
| **Security Engineer (Nemesis)** | Maintain encryption keys; monitor Lambda health; conduct annual penetration testing of the deletion workflow. |
| **Application Team** | Integrate the `faq‑deletion‑request` endpoint; ensure proper classification of incoming data. |
| **Operations** | Provision IAM roles; enforce MFA‑Delete on S3 buckets. |

## 9. Policy Enforcement  

- Non‑compliance with this policy may result in disciplinary action per the organization’s **Code of Conduct** (see `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/policies/code_of_conduct.md`).  
- Violations that lead to GDPR or HIPAA breaches will be reported to the appropriate supervisory authority within the statutory timeframes.
